criteria.label: Produce a histogram with KDE for one numeric column, split by a categorical hue.
criteria.example: On the penguins dataset, run `sns.histplot(data=penguins, x='flipper_length_mm', hue='species', kde=True)` and save the figure.

criteria.label: Build and annotate a Pearson correlation heatmap for numeric features.
criteria.example: Given a DataFrame with columns age, income, tenure, compute `corr = df[['age','income','tenure']].corr()` and plot `sns.heatmap(corr, annot=True, fmt='.2f', center=0)`.

criteria.label: Create a pair plot with corner layout and class-colored hue for EDA.
criteria.example: Run `sns.pairplot(iris, hue='species', corner=True, diag_kind='hist')` on three or four numeric iris columns and describe one visible class separation.

criteria.label: Explain when to use figure-level versus axes-level Seaborn functions.
criteria.example: State that `catplot(col='region')` is figure-level for faceting, while `sns.boxplot(..., ax=ax)` is axes-level for a custom subplot grid—and give one sentence why each fits its use case.
